title: physaria arenosa subsp. argillosa
intro: Physaria arenosa subsp. argillosa, also known as clay blomfoerder, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as its ability to support digestive health. The health benefits of Physaria arenosa subsp. argillosa include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
